(2) Authoritative source: the paper's Stim circuits are archived on Zenodo, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.18014961 (surface_code_encodings.zip), verified to exist. Caveat on the 'generate via generic stim/qiskit synthesis' suggestion: generic stabilizer-to-circuit synthesis will NOT reproduce the paper's specific hook-error-avoiding schedule (the CNOT-direction and pivot-selection constraints that make it distance-preserving); for the paper's exact circuits use the Zenodo files or transcribe the figures.
_correction: Two corrections, both minor; neither undermines the entry. (1) STEP COUNT: Fig. 3 is a 21-step encoder, not 22. The paper text states verbatim 'the 21 steps required in the ancilla-based version shown in Fig. 3' and the Fig. 3 caption says steps 1-21 perform the encoding while 'Step 22 depicts the whole set of stabilizers after the last gate is applied' — i.e. panel 22 is a final-state illustration, not a gate timestep. So the pair is 21-step (Fig. 3) vs 13-step (Fig. 4); the 13 is correct. (2) ATTRIBUTION: 'Zen et al.' is imprecise — Remmy Zen is the 2nd author; lead author is Luis Colmenarez (full list: Colmenarez, Zen, Olle, Marquardt, Mueller). Correct citation is 'Colmenarez et al.' The arXiv id 2601.05113, title, year 2026, code [[25,1,5]], gate set H/CNOT, |0>_L target, and ancilla-mediated-vs-intra-plaquette framing are all correct.

name: Steane [[7,1,3]] transversal logical Hadamard identity (H^{otimes7} = H_L)
paper: Error Correction Zoo Steane entry; orig. Shor (1997) transversal Clifford; Steane (1996)
figure_or_section: Error Correction Zoo c/steane 'Transversal gates' (single-qubit Clifford group transversal); Shor 1997
availability: generate via library: build H on all 7 qubits trivially in OpenQASM; the 'logical H' reference circuit = encoder . H_L-on-logical . decoder, constructible from the stabilizer tableau
why_good_benchmark: Clean transversality identity: applying physical H to all 7 qubits equals logical Hadamard because the code is self-dual CSS. Checkable as encoder-conjugated equivalence (H^{otimes7} acting after encoding == encoding of H|psi>). Trivial gate set, ideal smoke-test for the checker.
_subdomain: Surface code and color code purely-unitary operations (encoders / logical-state-prep, transversal and fold-transversal logical Clifford gates, unitary equivalents of lattice surgery)
_availability_verdict: Largely correct but the framing should be tightened. The transversal logical Hadamard IS just H applied to all 7 physical qubits — emit it directly (trivial OpenQASM/qiskit/Stim), no library magic needed. The "encoder . H_L-on-logical . decoder" wording in the claim describes the equivalence-CHECK, not the circuit itself, and is slightly misleading: the 'decoder' there must be the inverse of the unitary encoder (Clifford, no measurement), NOT a syndrome-measurement decoder. Both the bare H^otimes7 and the encoder/inverse-encoder used for the smoke test are purely unitary Clifford circuits, constructible from the stabilizer tableau via Stim or qiskit's StabilizerState/encoder utilities.
_correction: No substantive correction to the physics. (1) num_qubits=7, gate_set=H, self-dual CSS, X_L=X^otimes7, Z_L=Z^otimes7, and H^otimes7=H_L are all correct and standard (verified against Error Correction Zoo c/steane 'Transversal Gates' section, Wikipedia, Steane 1996 PRL 77,793 DOI 10.1103/PhysRevLett.77.793, Shor quant-ph/9605011, and the general result arXiv:2503.19790). (2) ECZoo does not literally print the string 'H^otimes7=H_L' but states 'transversal Hadamard acts logically on the code' and lists 'The single-qubit Clifford group' as transversal — fully consistent. (3) Minor availability wording fix as noted (decoder = inverse unitary encoder, not measurement-based). (4) arxiv 'unknown' is appropriate since the primary refs are a PRL and a quant-ph paper, not one arXiv id; the cited Shor 1997 corresponds to arXiv quant-ph/9605011.

name: Steane [[7,1,3]] transversal logical CNOT (CNOT^{otimes7} between two blocks = CNOT_L)
paper: Steane (1996); standard CSS transversal CNOT (Gottesman)
figure_or_section: Standard CSS result; Error Correction Zoo c/steane and c/css transversal-gate sections
availability: construct by hand: 7 transversal CX between matching qubits of the two blocks; reference logical CNOT built from two encoders + logical CX + two decoders via qiskit/stim
why_good_benchmark: Foundational transversality identity for CSS codes: 7 parallel physical CNOTs implement logical CNOT. Checkable as (E x E) then CX^{otimes7} equals CNOT_L then (E x E). 14 qubits is still tractable and exercises two-block reasoning.
_subdomain: Surface code and color code purely-unitary operations (encoders / logical-state-prep, transversal and fold-transversal logical Clifford gates, unitary equivalents of lattice surgery)
_availability_verdict: Confirmed and accurate. The circuit is built by hand: apply 7 physical CNOTs transversally between matching qubits i of block A (control) and block B (target), i = 0..6 — no measurement, reset, or classical control. To exhibit it as a "logical CNOT" reference, sandwich CX^{otimes7} between two Steane encoders (one per block) and two decoders, all of which are themselves unitary. Verification is straightforward in Qiskit/Stim/Cirq: check the stabilizer/logical-operator propagation (X_L (x) I -> X_L (x) X_L; I (x) Z_L -> Z_L (x) Z_L; I (x) X_L and Z_L (x) I preserved), or build the 14-qubit unitary and confirm (E (x) E) . CX^{otimes7} = CNOT_L . (E (x) E). All tractable at 14 qubits.
_correction: Two minor citation/labeling corrections, neither affecting validity. (1) The "figure_or_section" cites "Error Correction Zoo c/steane and c/css transversal-gate sections." The c/steane page DOES confirm the transversal Clifford group for the Steane code (refs Steane 1996; Shor 1997; Zeng-Cross-Chuang 2007), but the c/css page has NO transversal-gate section — the general "CSS codes admit transversal CNOT = logical CNOT" statement lives in Gottesman (1998), Nielsen & Chuang, and pedagogical sources, not on the zoo c/css page. (2) Attribution: best primary references are Steane, PRL 77, 793 (1996), DOI 10.1103/PhysRevLett.77.793, and Gottesman, "A Theory of Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computation," PRA 57, 127 (1998), arXiv:quant-ph/9702029 — so arxiv should be quant-ph/9702029 (Gottesman) rather than "unknown" (Steane 1996 predates arXiv-as-PRL but is real). The physics claim itself is exactly correct.

name: Surface code transversal logical CNOT between two patches (CNOT^{⊗d^2} = CNOT_L)
_availability_verdict: Confirmed and correct. The gate is trivially constructible by hand: apply a physical CNOT between every pair of corresponding/aligned data qubits of the two CSS surface-code patches (d^2 CNOTs = 9 for d=3, 25 for d=5). This is exactly what Sahay et al. 2408.01393 describe ("physical CNOT gates between every corresponding data qubit of the control and target SC states"). For the reference logical CNOT, build two surface-code encoders (e.g. via Stim/Qiskit), apply the transversal CX, and verify CNOT^{⊗d^2} acts as CNOT_L on the logical operators — this is the standard CSS transversal-CNOT identity (CNOT_L = CNOT^{⊗n} on aligned CSS codes). No special library is required for the unitary itself; the decoder/threshold machinery in the cited papers is separate and not part of the gate.
_correction: Minor attribution/labeling fixes, none affecting the circuit's validity: (1) The arXiv:2412.01391 author list is Zi-Han Chen, Ming-Cheng Chen, Chao-Yang Lu, Jian-Wei Pan (title: "Transversal Logical Clifford gates on rotated surface codes with reconfigurable neutral atom arrays") — the JSON variously calls this "Chen et al." (correct) but the top-level 'arxiv' field text says "Chen et al. completes Clifford set," which is fine; just note it provides transversal H and S, not the CNOT. (2) Sahay et al. 2408.01393 authors are Sahay, Lin, Huang, Brown, Puri; the name field's loose mention of "Cain et al. correlated decoding" refers to the genuinely real but separate paper arXiv:2403.03272 (Cain et al., PRL 133, 240602) — it is a decoding method, not where the gate is defined. (3) The transversal CNOT gate is purely unitary; the measurements in all cited papers belong to syndrome extraction/correlated decoding, NOT to the gate, so only the (clean) unitary part is the benchmark — this matches the claim. PRX Quantum 6, 020326 (2025) reference is exact.

name: Rotated surface code d=5 fold-transversal logical Hadamard (H^{otimes25} . diagonal-SWAP = H_L)
paper: Breuckmann & Burton, 'Fold-Transversal Clifford Gates for Quantum Codes'; Moussa folded surface codes
figure_or_section: Breuckmann-Burton Theorem 6, Fig. 1(i) (construction is distance-general)
availability: construct by hand (transversal H + diagonal-fold SWAPs on the 5x5 layout); reference H_L from stim/qiskit stabilizer tableau
why_good_benchmark: Same exact identity as d=3 but scaled to d=5 - tests that the checker handles the same transversality relation across a code family (scalability). 25 qubits, pure Clifford with a larger permutation.
_subdomain: Surface code and color code purely-unitary operations (encoders / logical-state-prep, transversal and fold-transversal logical Clifford gates, unitary equivalents of lattice surgery)
_availability_verdict: Confirmed: construct by hand. Apply transversal H on all 25 data qubits, then SWAP qubit pairs (i, tau(i)) across the diagonal fold axis of the 5x5 rotated layout; fixed points on the diagonal get H only. This is exactly the Breuckmann-Burton formula H_tau = (prod SWAP_{i,tau(i)}) . (prod H_i) = Moussa's folded-surface-code Hadamard. No library exports this exact circuit, so hand construction is the correct/only route; cross-check the logical action against a reference H_L via a stim or qiskit stabilizer tableau (verify it maps logical X<->Z and preserves the stabilizer group).
_correction: Citations and references are accurate; only minor wording caveats. (1) The cited "Theorem 6, Fig. 1(i)" is genuinely in Breuckmann-Burton (arXiv:2202.06647 v3): Thm 6 = "Given a ZX-duality tau for a CSS code C then H_tau is an encoded logical gate"; Fig 1(i) explicitly depicts Moussa's surface-code construction with tau reflecting along a diagonal, and the text states this reproduces "the Hadamard-gate described by Moussa." The formula H_tau = (prod SWAP) (prod H) matches the claim verbatim. (2) The parenthetical "construction is distance-general" is correct but is better attributed to Moussa (abstract: construction "presented generally," builds "families of folded surface codes") than to a specific B-B theorem. (3) The cited papers frame the fold on the (standard/diagonal-symmetric) surface code rather than using the word "rotated"; applying it to the d=5 [[25,1,5]]=[[L^2,1,L]] rotated patch is a correct application (the rotated patch has the required diagonal ZX-symmetry), not a verbatim quote. (4) num_qubits=25 = data qubits only, which is correct for the unitary. (5) Note transversal H alone exchanges X/Z stabilizers (reflects the patch); the diagonal SWAPs are what restore the original code, making it an in-place unitary automorphism.

name: Rotated surface code d=3 fold-transversal logical S gate (S/Sdag on diagonal + CZ on mirror pairs = S_L)
paper: Breuckmann & Burton, 'Fold-Transversal Clifford Gates for Quantum Codes'; Chen et al. neutral-atom realization
figure_or_section: Breuckmann-Burton Theorem 7 and Fig. 1(i); S_tau = (prod_{i in A} S_i)(prod_{j in B} Sdag_j)(prod CZ_{i,tau(i)})
availability: construct by hand from Theorem 7: S and Sdag distributed on the diagonal fixed-point qubits, CZ on each mirror pair across the diagonal; reference S_L from stabilizer tableau
why_good_benchmark: Explicit fold-transversal identity for logical S with a non-trivial mix of S, Sdag and CZ - a richer Clifford circuit than the H case. Pairs with the fold-transversal H to give the full single-qubit logical Clifford group on d=3 surface code; directly checkable against encoder-conjugated S_L.
_subdomain: Surface code and color code purely-unitary operations (encoders / logical-state-prep, transversal and fold-transversal logical Clifford gates, unitary equivalents of lattice surgery)
_availability_verdict: CORRECTED. The construction is real and obtainable, but cite Breuckmann-Burton THEOREM 7 (the formula S_tau = ⊗_{i∈A}S_i ⊗_{j∈B}S_j† ⊗_{i<τ(i)}CZ_{i,τ(i)}), NOT "Fig. 1(i)" — Fig 1(i) shows Moussa's ZX-duality/Hadamard fold on the surface code, not the S gate. To obtain the d=3 circuit: take the rotated [[9,1,3]] surface code, identify the diagonal fold axis (its fixed-point qubits get S/Sdag split so each X-check is evenly covered), apply CZ to each pair of qubits mirrored across the diagonal. This single S/Sdag/CZ layer realizes logical S_L as a purely-unitary fold-transversal gate (Moussa arXiv:1603.02286 originated the surface-code case; Breuckmann-Burton arXiv:2202.06647 generalize it; Chen et al. arXiv:2412.01391 give the d=3 rotated-surface specifics). Verify against an encoder-conjugated S_L from the stabilizer tableau. NOTE: Breuckmann-Burton do NOT work the d=3 rotated code as an explicit example (their worked code is Bring's [[30,8,3]]); the d=3 specifics come from Chen et al. / Moussa.
_correction: Two corrections, neither fatal: (1) WRONG FIGURE. "Fig. 1(i)" does NOT show the fold-transversal S gate. Fig 1 has three panels (i),(ii),(iii), all illustrating ZX-duality/Hadamard folds; panel (i) is specifically Moussa's Hadamard construction on a surface code. The S_tau phase-gate formula lives in Theorem 7 (text), which IS correctly cited. Drop the "Fig. 1(i)" reference. (2) The "Chen et al. neutral-atom realization" (arXiv:2412.01391) embeds the fold-transversal S INSIDE a syndrome-extraction round (reset + CNOT layers + ancilla measurement), so that *hardware scheme* is not purely unitary as a whole. The purely-unitary object is the S/Sdag/CZ layer itself (Moussa / Breuckmann-Burton Theorem 7); the claim should rely on Breuckmann-Burton/Moussa for the unitary, not on the Chen et al. measurement-embedded protocol. The core claim (S on fixed-point diagonal set A, Sdag on set B, CZ on mirror pairs = logical S, purely unitary) is correct and well-sourced.

name: Color code [[17,1,5]] / [[19,1,5]] transversal logical Clifford (H, S transversal on triangular patch)
code: 2D triangular color code [[17,1,5]] (4.8.8 / square-octagon) and [[19,1,5]] (6.6.6 / hexagonal)
paper: Bombin & Martin-Delgado color codes; Kubica & Beverland 'Universal transversal gates with color codes - a simplified approach'
figure_or_section: Kubica-Beverland Sec. on transversal R_n and Hadamard; triangular color code family [[ (3d^2+1)/4, 1, d ]]
availability: generate via library: build the [[17,1,5]] or [[19,1,5]] stabilizers (color-code plaquettes), synthesize encoder via stim/qiskit, apply transversal H / S on all data qubits; OpenQASM export. Lattice/stabilizer definitions in errorcorrectionzoo and panqec
why_good_benchmark: Self-dual 2D color codes give all logical Clifford gates transversally (transversal H = H on all qubits, transversal S = S/Sdag pattern). Provides a distance-5 family ([[17,1,5]] vs [[19,1,5]]) with the same logical-H/S transversality identities to check - tests scaling and two lattice geometries realizing equivalent logical gates.
_subdomain: Surface code and color code purely-unitary operations (encoders / logical-state-prep, transversal and fold-transversal logical Clifford gates, unitary equivalents of lattice surgery)
_availability_verdict: Confirmable but the proposed toolchain needs correction. Stabilizer/lattice definitions for the [[17,1,5]] (4.8.8 square-octagon) and [[19,1,5]] (6.6.6 hexagonal) triangular color codes are documented at errorcorrectionzoo.org/c/488_color and /c/color, and are buildable/simulatable in the color-code-stim package (github.com/seokhyung-lee/color-code-stim) and Stim. The "panqec" reference is plausible but I could NOT independently confirm panqec ships these exact triangular-color-code constructions, so do not rely on it as the sole source. Recommended concrete route: (1) take the X/Z plaquette stabilizers from errorcorrectionzoo or color-code-stim; (2) synthesize a Clifford encoder (e.g. via stim's tableau / from the stabilizer generators); (3) apply the transversal logical gates as pure unitaries: H_L = H on all n data qubits; S_L = the S/S-dagger pattern over the bipartite 2-coloring of data qubits (for d=5, d≡1 mod 4, so the transversal S — not S-dagger — convention applies on the 4.8.8 patch); CNOT_L = transversal CNOT between two code blocks; (4) export OpenQASM. All four are measurement-free, classical-control-free unitaries on a fixed code.
_correction: Two corrections, neither fatal. (1) PARAMETER-FORMULA MISMATCH: the stated family "[[(3d^2+1)/4,1,d]]" yields 19 for d=5 and is the 6.6.6 (hexagonal) formula ONLY; it does NOT generate the 17-qubit 4.8.8 code. The two codes obey different length formulas: 4.8.8 has n=(d^2-1)/2+d (=17 at d=5), 6.6.6 has n=(3d^2+1)/4 (=19 at d=5). Listing one formula for both codes is imprecise. (2) CITATION-ATTRIBUTION MISMATCH (the more important one): the purely-unitary, single-block 2D transversal Clifford property (H=H^{\\otimes n}, transversal S pattern, transversal CNOT) is properly attributed to Bombin & Martin-Delgado, quant-ph/0605138 ('Topological Quantum Distillation', abstract: 'implement the whole Clifford group of unitary operations in a fully topological manner'), which IS listed as the secondary citation. The primary citation Kubica-Beverland (arXiv:1410.0069) is mainly about HIGHER-dimensional transversal R_n gates and implements the Hadamard via CODE-SWITCHING (a measurement-based dimensional jump) to achieve universality — that Hadamard is explicitly NOT a purely-unitary single-block gate. So citing Kubica-Beverland's 'Hadamard' as the source of the 2D purely-unitary transversal H is a partial mismatch; the correct primary source for the unitary 2D claim is Bombin-Martin-Delgado. The transversal-S-vs-S-dagger d mod 4 rule and the H/S/CNOT transversality are independently corroborated (errorcorrectionzoo, arXiv:2301.08057).

name: Rotated surface code d=3 fold-transversal logical Hadamard (H^{otimes9} . diagonal-SWAP = H_L)
paper: Breuckmann & Burton, 'Fold-Transversal Clifford Gates for Quantum Codes' (generalizing Moussa, 'Transversal Clifford gates on folded surface codes')
figure_or_section: Breuckmann-Burton Theorem 6 and Fig. 1(i) (diagonal fold of surface code); H_tau = (prod SWAP_{i,tau(i)}) . (prod H_i)
availability: construct by hand from Theorem 6: transversal H on all 9 qubits then SWAPs across the diagonal fold axis (fixed qubits on the diagonal unswapped); reference H_L via stabilizer tableau in stim/qiskit
why_good_benchmark: Exact, explicitly stated identity H_L = (transversal H) . (diagonal reflection SWAPs). Two equivalent forms to check: the fold-transversal physical circuit vs the encoder-conjugated logical H. Small (9 qubits), pure Clifford, and the SWAP-permutation is precisely the up-to-permutation reasoning a checker should handle.
_subdomain: Surface code and color code purely-unitary operations (encoders / logical-state-prep, transversal and fold-transversal logical Clifford gates, unitary equivalents of lattice surgery)
_availability_verdict: Confirmed and constructible exactly as stated. From Breuckmann-Burton Theorem 6 the fold-transversal operator is H_tau = (prod_{i<tau(i)} SWAP_{i,tau(i)}) . (prod_i H_i), and the paper states that for the surface code with the diagonal ZX-duality of Fig. 1(i) this yields Moussa's logical Hadamard. Build by hand: apply transversal H to all 9 data qubits, then SWAP the qubit pairs reflected across the diagonal fold axis, leaving the 3 on-diagonal qubits fixed (Moussa: 'qudits along the fold are unpaired'). For qubits (d=2) the qudit phases/daggers in Moussa's general formula vanish, so it reduces cleanly to H + SWAP. Verify the logical action via a stabilizer tableau (stim Tableau / qiskit Clifford) by conjugating the [[9,1,3]] stabilizers and logicals: it sends X_L<->Z_L. Caveat to record when building: on the ROTATED code the diagonal fold maps the lattice to a reflected/rotated copy, so to literally restore the original qubit-layout one needs an extra relabeling/boundary deformation (cf. ETRI J. 2024 'Optimal execution of logical Hadamard...'; arXiv:2509.05212); the logical gate is nonetheless purely unitary and correct 'up to permutation'.
_correction: Two minor scope/precision notes, no fabrication found. (1) The claimed unqualified identity H_L = (transversal H).(diagonal-reflection SWAPs) is exactly a logical Hadamard only because the diagonal reflection has tau^2 = id; Breuckmann-Burton explicitly warn that 'H_tau is generally not simply a logical Hadamard' and 'in general we do not have H_tau^2 = I' unless tau^2 = id. The diagonal surface-code duality does satisfy tau^2=id, so the claim holds here, but the general statement should not be over-generalized. (2) Breuckmann-Burton Fig. 1(i) and Moussa's figures depict the standard square (unrotated) surface code lattice, not specifically the [[9,1,3]] ROTATED code (Surface-17). The same fold-transversal H construction applies to the d=3 rotated code with 9 data qubits and 3 fixed diagonal qubits, but the cited figures are not literally of the rotated layout; this is a citation-scope nuance, not an error. Citation numbering (Theorem 6, Fig. 1(i), the H_tau formula) all verified exact.

name: [[8,3,2]] transversal CCZ identity: (T/T-dagger)^{otimes 8} = logical CCZ
gate_set: T, T-dagger (the unitary block); compare against logical CCZ built from H, CNOT, T (Clifford+T)
paper: K. Menendez et al. (also Wang et al. 2309.09893); '[[8,3,2]] color code' / errorcorrectionzoo
figure_or_section: Transversal CCZ-bar = T0 T1d T2d T3 T4d T5 T6 T7d (stated explicitly in 2309.08663 main text and Fig. 6 of 2309.09893); transversal CZ-bar_12=S0 S2d S4d S6, CZ-bar_13=S0 S1d S4d S5, CZ-bar_23=S0 S1d S2d S3
availability: construct from figure (explicit Pauli/phase pattern given in text). Build two circuits: (A) Clifford encoder + transversal (T,T-dagger) pattern; (B) Clifford encoder + a logical CCZ decomposed in Clifford+T on the 3 logical qubits embedded in the 8-qubit code. Checker confirms A == B up to global phase.
why_good_benchmark: Classic transversality identity: a depth-1 transversal single-qubit-rotation layer equals a logical 3-qubit non-Clifford gate. Exactly the kind of two-equivalent-forms check Manjushri targets; Clifford+T, no measurement. Also yields three transversal-CZ (pure Clifford) identities for additional cheaper test cases.
_subdomain: Purely unitary (Clifford) parts of magic-state preparation and distillation
_availability_verdict: Construct from the primary source (arXiv:2309.08663, Section II), which gives the explicit pattern: CCZ-bar = T0 T1d T2d T3 T4d T5 T6 T7d, and CZ-bar_12 = S0 S2d S4d S6, CZ-bar_13 = S0 S1d S4d S5, CZ-bar_23 = S0 S1d S2d S3 with S = T^2 (all verified to match the claim exactly). The proposed two-circuit equivalence check is sound: (A) Clifford encoder for the [[8,3,2]] color code followed by the transversal (T,T-dagger)^{otimes 8} layer, vs (B) the same Clifford encoder followed by a logical CCZ decomposed in Clifford+T on the 3 logical qubits; compare A == B up to global phase. The encoder is Clifford/unitary, so the whole benchmark is unitary-vs-unitary. Do NOT cite this as 'Fig. 6 of 2309.09893' for the explicit pattern (see correction); cite 2309.08663 main text instead.
_correction: Two citation issues, neither fatal. (1) Author initial wrong: the primary paper's lead author is Daniel Honciuc Menendez, not 'K. Menendez' — use 'Honciuc Menendez et al.' (or just Menendez, Ray, Vasmer). (2) Figure attribution overstated: the explicit per-qubit T/T-dagger pattern is in 2309.08663 main text (Section II), NOT primarily in 'Fig. 6 of 2309.09893'. Wang et al. Fig. 6 is the FULL fault-tolerant one-bit-addition circuit whose caption explicitly lists measurements ('flag fault-tolerant measurement of X1X3 ... transversal ccz, destructive measurement of X2, destructive measurement of Z1 and Z3'); the transversal CCZ is only one dashed sub-region (a T/T-dagger layer) within that measurement-based circuit. Also note Fig. 6 exists only in the longer arXiv version of 2309.09893; the published Science Advances/PMC version has Figs. 1-5 only. The transversal CCZ in 2309.08663 is fully (not weakly) transversal and equals logical CCZ; the CZ-by-S patterns are correct (CZ derived from transversality of CCZ and Pauli X). Bottom line: the unitary circuit and patterns are genuine; only fix the author initial and the figure citation.

name: [[8,3,2]] non-fault-tolerant 3-qubit reference circuit for CCZ-conjugated-by-H (unencoded)
code: logical level (3 qubits) of the [[8,3,2]] one-bit-adder
paper: Y. Wang et al., 'Fault-Tolerant One-Bit Addition with the Smallest Interesting Colour Code'
figure_or_section: Fig. 7 (non-fault-tolerant 3-qubit circuit submitted to compiler) and Fig. 2 (logical CCZ conjugated by H); drop the terminal Z measurements to get the unitary
availability: construct from figure (full 3-qubit gate list with H, T, T-dagger, CNOT is drawn). Strip measurements for the unitary part.
why_good_benchmark: Small (3-qubit) and is the LOGICAL action that the 8-qubit transversal circuit above must equal once decoded; gives a compact ground-truth unitary to check the encoded version against. Multiple T-count decompositions of CCZ-conjugated-by-H exist (alternate forms).
_subdomain: Purely unitary (Clifford) parts of magic-state preparation and distillation
_availability_verdict: Construct-from-figure is correct, with a citation-edition caveat. Use the arXiv v-with-15-figures version (arXiv:2309.09893, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2309.09893). There, Fig. 7 "Non-fault-tolerant circuit as submitted to compiler" is the 3-qubit bare-physical-qubit reference: it draws a Clifford+T body (gates H, T, T-dagger and CNOTs; text confirms "five cnots and three measurements") followed by a terminal Z-basis measurement on each of the 3 wires. To obtain the unitary, transcribe the H/T/T-dagger/CNOT gate body and DROP the three terminal Z measurements -- exactly as the claim states. IMPORTANT EDITION CAVEAT: the figure numbers only match the arXiv preprint. In the published Science Advances version (DOI 10.1126/sciadv.ado9024) the paper has only Figs. 1-5; the CCZ-conjugated-by-H logical circuit is Fig. 1B and the bare-qubit decomposition is Fig. 1C there, NOT Fig. 2/Fig. 7. Anyone pulling from the journal PDF must use Fig. 1B/1C instead.
_correction: Mostly accurate; three minor corrections. (1) Gate set: the claim lists "H, S/T, T-dagger, CNOT" but Fig. 7 uses only H, T, T-dagger, CNOT -- no S gate appears; "Clifford+T" is fine as a label but the explicit S is over-specified. (2) Figure references hold ONLY for the arXiv edition. In the published Science Advances version the same content is Fig. 1B (CCZ-conjugated-by-H logical circuit) and Fig. 1C (3-qubit bare-qubit decomposition), since the journal version has just Figs. 1-5. The citation should specify "arXiv version" to avoid a figure-number mismatch. (3) Fig. 2 (arXiv) shows the *simplified* logical 3-qubit circuit (|+++>, an H on the third qubit, the simplified entangler) that results FROM replacing the Toffoli with a CCZ-conjugated-by-H -- the "CCZ conjugated by H" is the derivation described in the text, with Fig. 2 being its simplified drawn form, not a literal H-CCZ-H picture. None of these are fabrications; the underlying circuit, code, transversal-CCZ property, and 3-qubit count are all real and correctly described.

name: Meier-Eastin-Knill four-qubit-code distillation: transversal H^{otimes 4} = logical (H tensor H)*SWAP identity
purely_unitary: yes (the H^{otimes4} layer and the encoder are unitary; the distillation projector is excluded)
paper: A. M. Meier, B. Eastin, E. Knill, 'Magic-state distillation with the four-qubit code'
figure_or_section: Code def. (stabilizers XXXX, ZZZZ; logicals X1=XXII, Z1=ZIIZ, X2=XIIX, Z2=ZZII) in Sec. I; H^{otimes4} = logical H-tensor-H + logical SWAP stated; detection circuits Fig. 2(b); 10-to-2 routine Fig. 4(a)
availability: construct from figure / code data: build the [[4,2,2]] CSS encoder (CNOT+H, e.g. via MQT QECC from the small check matrix) and the H^{otimes4} layer. Check encoder . H^{otimes4} . encoder^{-1} == logical (H tensor H) followed by logical SWAP, up to global phase.
why_good_benchmark: Very small (4 qubits) yet exercises a non-trivial transversal-Clifford identity (transversal H implements logical H-tensor-H plus a SWAP) -- a clean, fast equivalence-check fixture with a known closed-form answer and multiple encoder realizations.
_subdomain: Purely unitary (Clifford) parts of magic-state preparation and distillation
_availability_verdict: Correct and constructible. The recipe is sound: C4 ([[4,2,2]]) is a CSS code, so a unitary encoder can be synthesized from CNOT+H. MQT QECC's CSS encoder synthesis is real (docs at mqt.readthedocs.io/projects/qecc/.../Encoders.html; functions depth_optimal_encoding_circuit / gate_optimal_encoding_circuit / heuristic_encoding_circuit take a CSS check matrix). Build the 4x4 check matrix from stabilizers XXXX/ZZZZ, synthesize the encoder, append the purely-unitary H^{otimes4} layer, and verify encoder . H^{otimes4} . encoder^{-1} == logical(H tensor H) followed by logical SWAP up to global phase (a known closed-form target). Note: the encoder/H^{otimes4} layer is what's purely unitary and worth using; do NOT try to obtain the full Fig 2(b)/Fig 4 routines as unitary circuits, since those are measurement-based.
_correction: Minor figure-caption nuance, not an error in the claim: Figure 4's published caption headline is "Illustration of a method of classifying errors in our distillation routine," but its panel (a) does show the full 10-to-2 distillation routine, so "10-to-2 routine Fig. 4(a)" is substantively correct. All other technical details verified verbatim against the paper (stabilizers XXXX/ZZZZ; logicals X1=XXII, Z1=ZIIZ, X2=XIIX, Z2=ZZII; and the exact statement that H^{otimes4} effects a logical Hadamard on both encoded qubits followed/preceded by a logical SWAP; Fig 2(b) = encoded detection circuit). The "purely_unitary: yes" label in the claim should be read as "yes for the extracted H^{otimes4}+encoder sub-part only" -- the claim itself already scopes this correctly by excluding the distillation projector, so it is partial overall but cleanly extractable.

_availability_verdict: Confirmed with a caveat. The unitary sub-part = CSS X-encoder (CNOT circuit read off the triorthogonal generator G, standard CSS encoding) + transversal T^{otimes n} + diagonal Clifford correction U built ONLY from CZ (Lambda(Z)) and S gates. This is a well-defined unitary satisfying the exact identity U T^{otimes n}|G> = |A^{otimes k}> (Eq. 6 / Lemma 2). MUST exclude the final non-destructive X-stabilizer measurement + postselect(s=0) + decoder shown in Fig. 1 (those are measurement/projection, not unitary). To obtain it: (a) build the CSS code from G and synthesize the encoder via MQT QECC's unitary encoder functions (gate_optimal_encoding_circuit / depth_optimal_encoding_circuit / heuristic synthesize_encoding_circuit) — these produce CNOT/H isometries with NO measurement — then export QASM via the underlying Qiskit circuit; (b) append T^{otimes n}; (c) append U as CZ^{Lambda_pq} on each pair p<q and (S^dagger)^{Lambda_p} on each qubit, where the integer coefficients Lambda_p, Lambda_pq come from Eq. (10). Caveat: MQT QECC's CSS encoder only gives the encoding isometry; the diagonal Clifford U is NOT emitted by MQT and must be constructed separately from Eq. (10), not "read off" automatically. _correction: Citation and all cited locators are accurate (verified against the full PDF): Fig. 1 IS the distillation subroutine (Encoder -> T^{otimes n} -> Z-errors -> Clifford U from Lemma 2 -> Measure X-syndrome s -> Postselect s=0 -> Decoder); Lemma 2 IS Eq. (6) stating U is composed of Lambda(Z) and S gates only with |A^{otimes k}> = U T^{otimes n}|G>; Eqs. (7)-(10) develop U with the explicit prescription "Lambda(Z)^{Lambda_pq} on every pair p<q and (S^dagger)^{Lambda_p} on every qubit"; Appendix B does present the [[49,1,5]] code. Minor clarifications, not errors in the claim: (1) the NEW general triorthogonal family constructed in this paper is the distance-2 [[3k+8,k,2]] family (Eq. 25, G(k)), while [[15,1,3]] (Reed-Muller, from Ref [13] Bravyi-Kitaev) and [[49,1,5]] (Appendix B) are the distance-3/5 examples used as benchmarks — the claim states this correctly. MQT QECC's synthesize_encoding_circuit(code) is a REAL function (verified in current QECC docs, mqt.readthedocs.io/projects/qecc/.../Encoders.html); it synthesizes a CNOT+H encoding circuit for a CSS code. (1) Source attribution of the transversal-T fact: the property that logical T is realized by T-dagger on every physical qubit is NOT stated in Bravyi-Kitaev (quant-ph/0403025) itself; the paper defines the 15-qubit code and a MEASUREMENT-based 15-to-1 distillation subroutine (measure stabilizers S1-S4, discard on nontrivial syndrome, apply Clifford decoder). The transversal-Tdg=logical-T statement is documented on errorcorrectionzoo.org/c/stab_15_1_3 (which cites Bravyi-Kitaev) and is standard/correct. (2) The artifact is a composite: the H+CNOT encoder and the single-qubit Tdg^{otimes15} layer are purely unitary and legitimately extractable, but the distillation ROUTINE in the cited paper is fundamentally measurement-and-postselection based, not unitary. The 'figure_or_section' field correctly notes the encoder is not drawn in the paper. The arxiv id quant-ph/0403025, title, authors, and PRA 71,022316 (2005) are all correct.

The recursive encoder has runnable reference code from the authors at https://github.com/Praveen91299/QRM (qrm_circuits_new.py, built with Tequila, visualized via Qiskit) implementing exactly this paper, so "construct from paper" undersells it. The standard/optimal pairing is real: MQT QECC (github.com/munich-quantum-toolkit/qecc) synthesizes encoders for CSS codes including the [[15,1,3]] Reed-Muller code via gate_optimal_encoding_circuit()/depth_optimal_encoding_circuit() (z3 SMT-optimal) and synthesize_encoding_circuit() (heuristic), producing purely unitary Clifford circuits (CNOT + single-qubit, no measurements/resets). One caveat: I could not directly confirm one-line QASM export for either tool from docs; MQT QECC returns Qiskit QuantumCircuit objects (qasm2/qasm3 dump straightforward) and the QRM repo uses Tequila (export to QASM/Qiskit is possible but may need a conversion step). _correction: Author attribution is slightly off: the claim cites "P. J. Nadkarni et al." but Nadkarni is the SECOND author; the first author is Praveen Jayakumar (full author list: Praveen Jayakumar, Priya J. Nadkarni, Shayan Srinivasa Garani). Title/arXiv id/year all match exactly. Second: the paper does NOT use the literal "[[15,1,3]]" notation or contain a figure labeled for it; it works in the general pQRM(0)(r,m) = [[2^m-1, 1, >=2^min(r+1,m-r)-1]] parametrization (proven near eq. 28, p.~"2.2.5 Punctured zero rate QRM codes"). The [[15,1,3]] instance is obtained by setting r=1, m=4 (giving distance 2^min(2,3)-1 = 3). The recursive encoder figures are Fig.~1-2 (general U(r,m) and "Recursive encoder for QRM(r,m)") and Figs.~4-9 in appendices for the punctured/state-prep variants; the [[15,1,3]] circuit is instantiated from these, not a standalone labeled figure -- the claim's figure_or_section was honest about this. No correction needed to the unitary or gate-set claims.

paper: J. Haah, M. B. Hastings, 'Codes and Protocols for Distilling T, controlled-S, and Toffoli Gates'
figure_or_section: Encoding described as 'prepare nX |+> ancillas and apply a Clifford' / 'circuit consisting of control-NOTs'; generalized-triorthogonal construction (Sec. on codes); RM example for T-to-Toffoli
availability: generate via library: the CSS X-encoder is a CNOT circuit read from the generator matrix; the diagonal Clifford correction (CZ/CCZ/S to land logical CS or Toffoli) is read from the generalized-triorthogonality data. Construct from matrices in the paper, export QASM. Excludes the X-stabilizer projector.
why_good_benchmark: Targets logical CS and Toffoli (CCZ) instead of T, so the transversal-T -> logical-multiqubit-gate identity is richer; the diagonal Clifford correction admits many commuting reorderings = alternate constructions of the same unitary.
_subdomain: Purely unitary (Clifford) parts of magic-state preparation and distillation
_availability_verdict: Confirmed and sound. Generate as the user describes: build the CSS X-encoder as a CNOT circuit read from the code's generator matrix G (the paper states the encoding "can be implemented by a circuit consisting of control-NOTs", Sec. 2.2), then append the diagonal Clifford correction (S/CZ) read from the generalized-triorthogonality data (Def. 1, Sec. 2.3; corrections noted in Sec. 2.2 as "either phase gate or control-Z"). Export to QASM. This unitary sub-circuit excludes the X-stabilizer projector / X-basis measurement and post-selection, which the full distillation protocol does require (Sec. 2.2). Matrices are constructible from the paper (RM-based construction in Sec. 4; the [[512,30,8]] 512-T-to-10-Toffoli example is in the abstract and Sec. 4). No public turnkey QASM export was located; it must be built from the matrices, as stated.
_correction:
Two minor corrections, neither fundamental. (1) The claim lists the correction gate set as "CZ, CCZ, S", but per the paper and the literature, Clifford corrections after transversal T are S (phase) and CZ only; CCZ is the transversal physical gate that induces the logical CCZ/Toffoli, not a correction. Moreover, for the CCZ/Toffoli target, Clifford corrections are generally NOT required (CCZ is "more natural" than T for these codes) -- corrections are mainly needed for the T target. So "CZ, CCZ, S" slightly overstates the correction set. (2) The encoder description conflates two passages: the literal "circuit of control-NOTs" creating a Bell pair embedded in code G (Sec. 2.2) and the "prepare nX |+> ancillas + apply a Clifford" space-time-tradeoff variant (Sec. 2.4). Both exist in the paper; neither is fabricated. The figure_or_section pointers are paraphrases but accurately locate the content (Sec. 2.2-2.3 for the construction, Sec. 4 for the RM/[[512,30,8]] example).</correction>
</invoke>

name: Litinski 20-to-4 distillation circuit (block-code, 24-rotation identity form)
gate_set: H, CNOT, S (Clifford skeleton) + T rotations
paper: D. Litinski, 'Magic State Distillation: Not as Costly as You Think'
figure_or_section: Fig. 4 (20-to-4 circuit; uses a 24-rotation sequence non-trivially equal to identity, outputs |m-tilde>^{4} tensor |+>^{3})
availability: construct from figure (rotation list given). Expand each Pauli pi/8 rotation into CNOT-ladder + T; remove terminal measurements for the unitary part.
why_good_benchmark: The 24-rotation sequence is explicitly stated to be 'non-trivially equivalent to the identity' on the check qubits -> a direct nontrivial-identity test for an equivalence checker, and part of a scalable (3k+8)-to-k family.
_subdomain: Purely unitary (Clifford) parts of magic-state preparation and distillation
_availability_verdict: Confirmed and refined. The circuit is NOT shipped as a ready-to-run unitary anywhere. The GitHub supplement (github.com/litinski/magicstates, file Python/twolevel20to4.py) only performs arbitrary-precision density-matrix cost/error simulations: it applies the Pauli pi/8 rotations as e^{-iP pi/8} operators and then post-selects by projecting check qubits 5-7 onto |+> (projx) — it does not emit a gate-level circuit. To obtain the purely-unitary part you must construct it from Fig. 4's explicit rotation list (the 20-rotation sequence on the 7-qubit working register), expand each Pauli pi/8 rotation into a CNOT-ladder + single T, and drop the terminal X-basis check measurements on qubits 5-7. Equivalently, the cleanest pure-identity benchmark is the 24-rotation sequence that is stated to be non-trivially equal to the identity (Fig. 4 minus the 4 magic-state-injection rotations / the construction described at lines around the Fig.4 text), which is a genuine unitary identity-equivalence test. The stated availability path in the claim is therefore correct.
_correction: Minor: (a) The (3k+8)-to-k family is real (verbatim in the paper: "the 20-to-4 protocol is part of an entire family of (3k + 8)-to-k protocols ... space cost governed by k+3, time cost by 3k+8, space-time minima at k=2 and k=4"), so k=4 -> 20-to-4 is the correct instance. (b) Qubit count: the working register the rotation circuit acts on is 7 qubits (outputs |m-tilde>^4 tensor |+>^3, with qubits 5-7 as check qubits projected onto |+>), confirmed by the supplement code kron(one,one,one,one,projx,projx,projx); the "20" in 20-to-4 counts consumed input magic states (injected via rotations), not register width. (c) The verbatim caption is "Figure 4: 20-to-4 distillation circuit." and the verbatim text is "using a 24-rotation circuit that non-trivially corresponds to the identity, a 20-rotation circuit that outputs a |m-tilde>^{4} tensor |+>^{3} state can be obtained. This is the 20-to-4 protocol [11] shown in Fig. 4." All claim wording matches the source. No fabrication found.

name: Single-qubit magic-state prep: |H> = T|+>, |Y> = S|+>, |T> rotation (and Clifford+T equivalents)
gate_set: H, S, T (and RY/RZ in the ideal-rotation form)
paper: S. Bravyi, A. Kitaev, 'Universal quantum computation with ideal Clifford gates and noisy ancillas' (defns); cross-ref C. Chamberland, A. Cross 1811.00566 (|H> usage)
figure_or_section: |H> = cos(pi/8)|0>+sin(pi/8)|1> = +1 eigenstate of H, preparable as H then T on |0> (i.e. T|+>); |Y> = S|+> = (|0>+i|1>)/sqrt2 (Y/S eigenstate, a stabilizer state)
availability: generate via library (qiskit: h;t for |H>, h;s for |Y>) -> trivial QASM. Multiple equivalent decompositions (e.g. |Y>=S|+>=HSH...|0> variants; |H>=T|+> vs RY(pi/4)|0> up to phase).
why_good_benchmark: Smallest possible non-trivial fixtures: |Y>=S|+> is a stabilizer state with many Clifford-equivalent prep circuits (great for global-phase handling), and |H>=T|+> gives the minimal Clifford+T target. Useful as base cases / regression tests before the multi-qubit codes.
_subdomain: Purely unitary (Clifford) parts of magic-state preparation and distillation
_availability_verdict: Confirmed trivially generatable via library/QASM. CAVEAT: the qiskit `h;t` sequence produces T|+> = (|0>+e^{iπ/4}|1>)/√2 (the |A> / T-injection state), NOT the Bravyi-Kitaev |H> eigenstate. To actually prepare the |H> magic state cos(π/8)|0>+sin(π/8)|1> use RY(π/4) on |0> (verified to match BK Def. 1 ρ_H), or a Clifford+T sequence such as H;T;H up to phase. `h;s` correctly yields |Y> = S|+> = (|0>+i|1>)/√2. Label the QASM by the actual state produced.
_correction: Two corrections. (1) MATH ERROR: "|H> = T|+>" is FALSE. T|+> = (|0>+e^{iπ/4}|1>)/√2 has Bloch vector (1/√2,1/√2,0) (equatorial XY-plane; this is the |A>/T-injection state) and is NOT an eigenstate of H. The actual BK |H> = cos(π/8)|0>+sin(π/8)|1> has Bloch vector (1/√2,0,1/√2) (XZ-plane, +1 eigenstate of Hadamard). Verified numerically: H|H>=|H> True; ρ(T|+>)=ρ_H False; RY(π/4)|0> matches ρ_H True. The "minimal Clifford+T target" the finder describes (T|+>) is real and useful but is the |A> state, not |H>. (2) ATTRIBUTION: Bravyi-Kitaev Definition 1 (p.3 of quant-ph/0403025) defines ONLY |H> and |T> via the density matrices ½[I+(σx+σz)/√2] and ½[I+(σx+σy+σz)/√3]. There is no "|Y> magic state" in that paper; |Y>=S|+> is a stabilizer state (not a magic state, Clifford-preparable), so attributing it to BK magic-state definitions is loose — it should be flagged as a stabilizer fixture, not a BK magic state. The +1-eigenstate-of-H characterization of |H> is correctly attributable (BK state it explicitly).
